# Parser implementation for command line input (2026-03-13 05:54Z)

_**User**_

Implement Task 13 from docs/plans/2026-03-13-dev-shell-impl-plan.md.

**Task 13: 解析器 — 行拆分为命令与重定向（TDD）**

1. Create tests/parser_tests.rs:
   use dev_shell::parser::{parse_line, Pipeline, SimpleCommand, Redirect};
   #[test] fn parse_simple() {
     let p = parse_line("echo hello").unwrap();
     assert_eq!(p.commands.len(), 1);
     assert_eq!(p.commands[0].argv, ["echo", "hello"]);
   }
   #[test] fn parse_redirect_out() {
     let p = parse_line("echo hi > out").unwrap();
     assert_eq!(p.commands[0].redirects.len(), 1);
     assert_eq!(p.commands[0].redirects[0].path, "out");
     // fd for > is 1
   }

2. In src/lib.rs add: pub mod parser;

3. Create src/parser.rs:
   pub struct Redirect { pub fd: u8; pub path: String; }  // fd: 0=stdin, 1=stdout, 2=stderr
   pub struct SimpleCommand { pub argv: Vec<String>; pub redirects: Vec<Redirect>; }
   pub struct Pipeline { pub commands: Vec<SimpleCommand>; }
   pub fn parse_line(line: &str) -> Result<Pipeline, ParseError>.
   Tokenize by splitting on spaces but respect that > 2> < and | are special. So tokenize: split on whitespace, but treat > 2> < | as separate tokens. Then: split by | to get commands; for each command collect argv until we see > 2> < then next token is path (fd 1, 2, 0). Return Pipeline { commands }.

4. Run cargo test parse. Commit: git add src/parser.rs src/lib.rs tests/parser_tests.rs && git commit -m "feat(parser): parse line to pipeline with redirects"
